_meta.purpose: Raw mlx_lm.generate string outputs for MLXAdapter unit tests. Each entry's `raw` field is what mlx_lm.generate returns; tests mock generate to return that string and assert ProviderResponse correctness per HARNESS_SPEC §4 (MLX) and §5 acceptance items 1-4.
_meta.envelope: <tool_call>{json}</tool_call> (DOTALL regex). enable_thinking=False so no leading <think> blocks.

clean_tool_call.description: Spec §4 MLX #1 — model emits a well-formed envelope for a registry-resident tool. Adapter parses one ToolCall; runner classifies as `executed`.

hallucinated_tool_call.description: Spec §4 MLX #2 — well-formed envelope but `name` not in registry. Adapter MUST surface it verbatim; runner's RVR classifies as hallucinated.

refusal_no_tool.description: Spec §4 MLX #3 — plain refusal text, no envelope. parse_ok stays True (legitimate no-tool-call path); runner deny-list classifies as refused.
refusal_no_tool.raw: I'm sorry, I can't do that.

expected.raw_text: I'm sorry, I can't do that.

parse_fail_malformed.description: Spec §4 MLX #4 — envelope matches the regex but the inner JSON is malformed (`{oops}` is not valid JSON). Adapter returns parse_ok=False with empty tool_calls; runner classifies as parse_fail.
